   144→
   145→    private func notifyFullscreenTransitionCompleted() {
   146→        DebugCentering.log("notifyFullscreenTransitionCompleted dispatch")
   147→        Task { @MainActor [weak self] in
   148→            guard let self,
   149→                  let vc = self.window?.contentViewController as? ImageViewController else { return }
   150→            DebugCentering.log("notifyFullscreenTransitionCompleted invoke VC handler")
   151→            vc.handleFullscreenTransitionDidComplete()
   152→        }
   153→    }
   154→
   155→    @objc private func windowDidResizeNotification(_ notification: Notification) {
   156→        guard let window = notification.object as? NSWindow,
   157→              !window.styleMask.contains(.fullScreen),
   158→              !isTransitioningFullScreen else { return }
   159→
   160→        Task { @MainActor [weak self] in
   161→            guard let self,
   162→                  let vc = self.window?.contentViewController as? ImageViewController else { return }
   163→            vc.handleWindowDidResize()
   164→        }
   165→
   166→        resizeSaveTask?.cancel()
   167→        let size = window.contentView?.bounds.size ?? window.frame.size
   168→        guard size.width >= Constants.minWindowContentWidth,
   169→              size.height >= Constants.minWindowContentHeight else {
   170→            return
   171→        }
   172→        let task = DispatchWorkItem {
   173→            var settings = ViewerSettings.load()
   174→            settings.lastWindowWidth = size.width
   175→            settings.lastWindowHeight = size.height
   176→            settings.save()
   177→        }
   178→        resizeSaveTask = task
   179→        DispatchQueue.main.asyncAfter(deadline: .now() + 0.5, execute: task)
   180→    }
   181→
   182→    private func ensureUsableWindowSize() {
   183→        guard let window else { return }
   184→        DispatchQueue.main.async {
   185→            let size = window.contentView?.bounds.size ?? window.frame.size
   186→            let minContentSize = self.effectiveMinimumContentSize()
   187→            guard size.width < minContentSize.width ||
   188→                  size.height < minContentSize.height else { return }
   189→            window.setContentSize(Self.defaultWindowSize())
   190→            window.center()
   191→        }
   192→    }
   193→
   194→    func effectiveMinimumContentSize() -> NSSize {
   195→        guard let window else {
   196→            return Constants.minWindowContentSize
   197→        }
   198→
   199→        return Self.effectiveMinimumContentSize(
   200→            configuredMinContentSize: window.contentMinSize,
   201→            layoutMinContentSize: window.contentView?.fittingSize ?? .zero
   202→        )
   203→    }
   204→
   205→    static func effectiveMinimumContentSize(
   206→        configuredMinContentSize: NSSize,
   207→        layoutMinContentSize: NSSize
   208→    ) -> NSSize {
   209→        NSSize(
   210→            width: max(
   211→                Constants.minWindowContentWidth,
   212→                configuredMinContentSize.width,
   213→                layoutMinContentSize.width
   214→            ),
   215→            height: max(
   216→                Constants.minWindowContentHeight,
   217→                configuredMinContentSize.height,
   218→                layoutMinContentSize.height
   219→            )
   220→        )
   221→    }

   223→    // MARK: - Resize to Fit Image
   224→
   225→    func resizeToFitImage(_ size: NSSize, center: Bool = true) {
   226→        guard let window,
   227→              let screen = window.screen,
   228→              !window.styleMask.contains(.fullScreen) else { return }
   229→        let visibleFrame = screen.visibleFrame
   230→        let maxSize = visibleFrame.size
   231→        let requestedSize = NSSize(
   232→            width: min(size.width, maxSize.width),
   233→            height: min(size.height, maxSize.height)
   234→        )
   235→        let minimumContentSize = effectiveMinimumContentSize()
   236→        let effectiveMinContent = NSSize(
   237→            width: min(minimumContentSize.width, maxSize.width),
   238→            height: min(minimumContentSize.height, maxSize.height)
   239→        )
   240→        let targetSize = NSSize(
   241→            width: max(requestedSize.width, effectiveMinContent.width),
   242→            height: max(requestedSize.height, effectiveMinContent.height)
   243→        )
   244→        let currentCenter = NSPoint(x: window.frame.midX, y: window.frame.midY)
   245→        let targetFrameSize = window.frameRect(
   246→            forContentRect: NSRect(origin: .zero, size: targetSize)
   247→        ).size
   248→        var targetFrame = NSRect(origin: .zero, size: targetFrameSize)
   249→
   250→        if center {
   251→            targetFrame.origin = NSPoint(
   252→                x: visibleFrame.midX - targetFrame.width / 2.0,
   253→                y: visibleFrame.midY - targetFrame.height / 2.0
   254→            )
   255→        } else {
   256→            targetFrame.origin = NSPoint(
   257→                x: currentCenter.x - targetFrame.width / 2.0,
   258→                y: currentCenter.y - targetFrame.height / 2.0
   259→            )
   260→        }
   261→
   262→        targetFrame.origin = clampedWindowOrigin(
   263→            for: targetFrame,
   264→            within: visibleFrame
   265→        )
   266→        if DebugCentering.isEnabled {
   267→            let before = window.frame
   268→            DebugCentering.log(
   269→                "resizeToFitImage before frame=\(String(format: "%.1f,%.1f %.1f×%.1f", before.origin.x, before.origin.y, before.width, before.height)) " +
   270→                "requested=\(String(format: "%.1f×%.1f", requestedSize.width, requestedSize.height)) " +
   271→                "minContent=\(String(format: "%.1f×%.1f", effectiveMinContent.width, effectiveMinContent.height)) " +
   272→                "target=\(String(format: "%.1f,%.1f %.1f×%.1f", targetFrame.origin.x, targetFrame.origin.y, targetFrame.width, targetFrame.height)) " +
   273→                "center=\(center)"
   274→            )
   275→        }
   276→        window.setFrame(targetFrame, display: true, animate: false)
   277→        if DebugCentering.isEnabled {
   278→            let after = window.frame
   279→            DebugCentering.log(
   280→                "resizeToFitImage after frame=\(String(format: "%.1f,%.1f %.1f×%.1f", after.origin.x, after.origin.y, after.width, after.height))"
   281→            )
   282→        }
   283→    }
   284→
   285→    func fillWindowHeight() {
   286→        guard let window,
   287→              let screen = window.screen,
   288→              !window.styleMask.contains(.fullScreen) else { return }
   289→        let targetFrame = Self.expandedHeightFrame(from: window.frame, within: screen.visibleFrame)
   290→        window.setFrame(targetFrame, display: true, animate: false)
   291→    }
   292→
   293→    static func expandedHeightFrame(from frame: NSRect, within visibleFrame: NSRect) -> NSRect {
   294→        let width = min(frame.width, visibleFrame.width)
   295→        let maxX = max(visibleFrame.maxX - width, visibleFrame.minX)
   296→        let originX = min(max(frame.minX, visibleFrame.minX), maxX)
   297→        return NSRect(
   298→            x: originX,
   299→            y: visibleFrame.minY,
   300→            width: width,
   301→            height: visibleFrame.height
   302→        )
   303→    }
   304→
   305→    private func clampedWindowOrigin(for frame: NSRect, within visibleFrame: NSRect) -> NSPoint {
   306→        let minX = visibleFrame.minX
   307→        let minY = visibleFrame.minY
   308→        let maxX = max(visibleFrame.maxX - frame.width, minX)
   309→        let maxY = max(visibleFrame.maxY - frame.height, minY)
   310→
   311→        return NSPoint(
   312→            x: min(max(frame.origin.x, minX), maxX),
   313→            y: min(max(frame.origin.y, minY), maxY)
   314→        )
   315→    }
   316→
   317→    // MARK: - Phase 2: Continuous Scroll Animated Resize
   318→
   319→    private var resizeDisplayLink: CADisplayLink?
   320→    private var resizeAnimationStartFrame: NSRect = .zero
   321→    private var resizeAnimationTargetSize: NSSize = .zero
   322→    private var resizeAnimationStartTime: CFTimeInterval = 0
   323→    private var resizeAnimationDuration: CFTimeInterval = 0.25
   324→    private var resizeAnimationPreserveCenter: Bool = true
   325→
   326→    /// 使用 CADisplayLink 執行動態視窗 resize（macOS 14+）
   327→    /// - Parameters:
   328→    ///   - targetSize: 目標視窗大小
   329→    ///   - preserveCenter: 是否保持視窗中心點位置
   330→    func animateResize(to targetSize: NSSize, preserveCenter: Bool = true) {
   331→        guard let window = window else { return }
   332→
   333→        // 全螢幕模式下跳過 resize
   334→        guard !window.styleMask.contains(.fullScreen) else { return }
   335→
   336→        // 連續捲動模式下跳過 resize（使用 fit-to-width，固定寬度）
   337→        if let vc = contentViewController as? ImageViewController, vc.settings.continuousScrollEnabled {
   338→            return
   339→        }
   340→
   341→        // 建立或重用 display link
   342→        let displayLink: CADisplayLink
   343→        if let existing = resizeDisplayLink {
   344→            displayLink = existing
   345→        } else {
   346→            displayLink = window.displayLink(target: self, selector: #selector(resizeAnimationStep(_:)))
   347→            resizeDisplayLink = displayLink
   348→        }
   349→
   350→        // 設定動畫參數
   351→        resizeAnimationStartFrame = window.frame
   352→        resizeAnimationTargetSize = targetSize
   353→        resizeAnimationStartTime = CACurrentMediaTime()
   354→        resizeAnimationPreserveCenter = preserveCenter
   355→
   356→        displayLink.isPaused = false
   357→    }
   358→
   359→    @objc private func resizeAnimationStep(_ link: CADisplayLink) {
   360→        guard let window = window else {
   361→            link.invalidate()
   362→            resizeDisplayLink = nil
   363→            return
   364→        }
   365→
   366→        let elapsed = CACurrentMediaTime() - resizeAnimationStartTime
   367→        let progress = min(1.0, elapsed / resizeAnimationDuration)
   368→
   369→        // Ease-in-out 曲線
   370→        let easedProgress = easeInOut(progress)
   371→
   372→        // 插值計算當前 size
   373→        let currentSize = interpolateSize(
   374→            from: resizeAnimationStartFrame.size,
   375→            to: resizeAnimationTargetSize,
   376→            progress: easedProgress
   377→        )
   378→
   379→        var currentFrame: NSRect
   380→        if resizeAnimationPreserveCenter {
   381→            currentFrame = centeredFrame(for: currentSize, relativeTo: resizeAnimationStartFrame)
   382→        } else {
   383→            currentFrame = NSRect(
   384→                x: resizeAnimationStartFrame.origin.x,
   385→                y: resizeAnimationStartFrame.origin.y,
   386→                width: currentSize.width,
   387→                height: currentSize.height
   388→            )
   389→        }
   390→
   391→        // 螢幕邊界限制
   392→        if let screen = window.screen {
   393→            currentFrame = clampToScreen(currentFrame, screen: screen)
   394→        }
   395→
   396→        window.setFrame(currentFrame, display: true)
   397→
   398→        // 動畫完成
   399→        if progress >= 1.0 {
   400→            link.isPaused = true
   401→            link.invalidate()
   402→            resizeDisplayLink = nil
   403→        }
   404→    }
   405→
   406→    private func easeInOut(_ t: CGFloat) -> CGFloat {
   407→        return t < 0.5
   408→            ? 2 * t * t
   409→            : 1 - pow(-2 * t + 2, 2) / 2
   410→    }
   411→
   412→    private func interpolateSize(from: NSSize, to: NSSize, progress: CGFloat) -> NSSize {
   413→        NSSize(
   414→            width: from.width + (to.width - from.width) * progress,
   415→            height: from.height + (to.height - from.height) * progress
   416→        )
   417→    }
   418→
   419→    private func centeredFrame(for newSize: NSSize, relativeTo current: NSRect) -> NSRect {
   420→        let cx = current.midX
   421→        let cy = current.midY
   422→        return NSRect(
   423→            x: cx - newSize.width / 2,
   424→            y: cy - newSize.height / 2,
   425→            width: newSize.width,
   426→            height: newSize.height
   427→        )
   428→    }
   429→
   430→    private func clampToScreen(_ frame: NSRect, screen: NSScreen) -> NSRect {
   431→        let screenFrame = screen.visibleFrame
   432→        var clampedFrame = frame
   433→
   434→        // 確保視窗不超出螢幕
   435→        if frame.maxX > screenFrame.maxX {
   436→            clampedFrame.origin.x = screenFrame.maxX - frame.width
   437→        }
   438→        if frame.minX < screenFrame.minX {
   439→            clampedFrame.origin.x = screenFrame.minX
   440→        }
   441→        if frame.maxY > screenFrame.maxY {
   442→            clampedFrame.origin.y = screenFrame.maxY - frame.height
   443→        }
   444→        if frame.minY < screenFrame.minY {
   445→            clampedFrame.origin.y = screenFrame.minY
   446→        }
   447→
   448→        return clampedFrame
   449→    }
   450→
   451→    // MARK: - Window Title
   452→
   453→    func updateTitle(folder: ImageFolder?) {
   454→        guard let folder else {
   455→            window?.title = "Cee"
   456→            window?.subtitle = ""
   457→            return
   458→        }
   459→        guard let item = folder.currentImage else {
   460→            window?.title = "Cee"
   461→            window?.subtitle = ""
   462→            return
   463→        }
   464→        // StatusBar 可見時索引在 bar 顯示，標題列不重複
   465→        let showIndex = (contentViewController as? ImageViewController)
   466→            .map { !$0.settings.showStatusBar } ?? true
   467→        if showIndex {
   468→            let position = "\(folder.currentIndex + 1)/\(folder.images.count)"
   469→            window?.title = "\(item.url.lastPathComponent) (\(position))"
   470→        } else {
   471→            window?.title = item.url.lastPathComponent
   472→        }
   473→        window?.subtitle = item.pdfPageIndex.map { String(localized: "window.pdfPage \($0 + 1)") } ?? ""
   474→    }
   475→}

content.text:
I now have enough context to provide a thorough review. Let me summarize my findings.

---

## Plan Summary (Restated)

1. **Refactor StatusBarView horizontal layout**: Remove centerX constraint on sizeLabel, lower zoomLabel compression resistance to `.defaultLow`, use left-aligned sizeLabel + spacer layout so zoomLabel no longer inflates fittingSize.
2. **Add display mode switching** (`regular`/`compact`/`minimal`): Override `layout()` with stored `currentDisplayMode`, guard against infinite loops, apply hysteresis (20pt) for transitions, deactivate zoomLabel constraints in minimal mode.
3. **Compact zoom text via ZoomStatusStyle enum**: Add `full`/`compactPercentOnly` variants to `ZoomStatusFormatter`; StatusBarView stores current `ZoomStatusMode` to re-format on mode switch.
4. **Preserve existing window/zoom clamp logic**: No changes to `effectiveMinimumContentSize`, resize clamp, magnification floor.
5. **fittingSize target < 300**: Ensure all zoomLabel-related horizontal constraints are below `.required` priority so fittingSize drops below 300.
6. **Modify StatusBarView.swift and ZoomStatusFormatter only**: Minimal/no changes to ImageViewController or window/zoom logic.
7. **Update existing tests + add new tests**: Change `testStatusBarManualZoomTextExpandsFittingWidth` assertion from `> 500` to `< 350`, update `testEffectiveMinimumContentSize_prefersLayoutFittingWidthWhenLarger` with lower layoutMinContentSize, add display mode boundary tests and formatter compact output tests.

## Validation vs Codebase

### Affected Files (confirmed paths)
- `/Users/pc035860/code/cee/Cee/Views/StatusBarView.swift` — primary target, contains `ZoomStatusMode`, `ZoomStatusFormatter`, and `StatusBarView`
- `/Users/pc035860/code/cee/CeeTests/ImageViewControllerTests.swift` — existing tests to update
- `/Users/pc035860/code/cee/CeeTests/ImageWindowControllerTests.swift` — existing test to update

### Existing Patterns Verified
- The `statusBarHorizontalConstraints` deactivation pattern in `ImageViewController.applyStatusBar()` (line 258) already handles the CLAUDE.md fittingSize gotcha for the status bar container — this plan applies the same principle at a finer granularity (zoomLabel within StatusBarView).
- `ZoomStatusMode` and `ZoomStatusFormatter` are both in `StatusBarView.swift` (not separate files), so the plan's Step 6 file list is accurate.
- `effectiveMinimumContentSize()` at `/Users/pc035860/code/cee/Cee/Controllers/ImageWindowController.swift:194-221` takes `window.contentView?.fittingSize` — this is the contentView of the NSWindow (the container view created in `loadView()`), which propagates fittingSize from all subviews including StatusBarView. Lowering StatusBarView's fittingSize will directly reduce this value.

### Interface/Contract Check
- `StatusBarView.update(index:total:zoomMode:imageSize:indexOverride:)` is called from `ImageViewController` lines 276, 294, 302. The plan preserves this API, only adding internal state storage for `zoomMode`.
- `StatusBarView.updateZoom(_:)` is called at line 327. The plan preserves this API.
- `ZoomStatusFormatter.text(for:)` is a static method — adding a `style:` parameter with a default value of `.full` maintains backward compatibility with existing callers.

---

## Verdict

**PASS_WITH_CHANGES**

## Critical issues (must fix)

None. The first review's critical issues have been adequately addressed.

## Plan patch (proposed edits)

### 1. Clarify constraint deactivation scope in minimal mode (Step 2, clarification)

Step 2 says "deactivate `zoomLabel` 的水平約束" in minimal mode. This is correct in principle, but the implementation needs to be precise: the constraints to deactivate are zoomLabel's **leading/trailing** anchors (positional constraints), not its intrinsic content size constraints. Additionally, `indexLabel.trailingAnchor == zoomLabel.leadingAnchor - 12` (the current inter-label spacing constraint) must also be deactivated or replaced, otherwise deactivating zoomLabel's trailing anchor alone creates an ambiguous layout. 

**Proposed edit**: In Step 2, add a bullet:
- "When entering minimal mode, deactivate both the zoomLabel trailing anchor and the indexLabel-to-zoomLabel spacing constraint. Replace indexLabel's trailing constraint with a direct `indexLabel.trailingAnchor <= trailingAnchor - 8`. Store these as separate constraint groups (e.g., `zoomLabelConstraints` vs `minimalModeIndexTrailingConstraint`) for clean activate/deactivate."

### 2. Add default parameter to ZoomStatusFormatter.text (Step 3, minor)

**Proposed edit**: In Step 3, explicitly note that the new `style` parameter should have a default value of `.full`:
- `static func text(for mode: ZoomStatusMode, style: ZoomStatusStyle = .full) -> String`

This ensures all 4 existing call sites (`ImageViewControllerTests` x3 + `StatusBarView.updateZoom`) need zero changes.

### 3. Clarify hysteresis direction semantics (Step 2, clarification)

The plan says "從 regular->compact 的閾值比 compact->regular 小 20pt." This phrasing is ambiguous — "smaller threshold" could mean the width threshold is 20pt lower (which is correct: you need to shrink further to trigger compaction) or 20pt less (which is the same thing, but reads oddly). 

**Proposed edit**: Rephrase to: "Hysteresis: `regular->compact` triggers at width W, `compact->regular` triggers at width W+20pt. This means the view must grow 20pt wider than the compact threshold before it expands back to regular."

### 4. Consider `needsLayout` guard instead of pure mode-equality guard (Step 2, robustness)

The plan uses `guard newMode != currentDisplayMode else { return }` as the infinite loop guard. This is sufficient for preventing oscillation since the mode is stored and compared. However, there is a subtle risk: if constraint activation/deactivation in `applyDisplayMode()` causes a layout pass that re-enters `layout()`, the mode will already be set, so the guard catches it. This is correct. No change needed, but worth noting explicitly that `applyDisplayMode()` must set `currentDisplayMode = newMode` **before** activating/deactivating constraints.

**Proposed edit**: In Step 2, add:
- "In `applyDisplayMode()`, set `currentDisplayMode = newMode` BEFORE activating/deactivating constraints, since constraint changes trigger synchronous layout passes that re-enter `layout()`."

### 5. Add note about fittingSize measurement timing in tests (Step 7)

**Proposed edit**: In Step 7, add:
- "After calling `update(...)` on the test StatusBarView, call `layoutSubtreeIfNeeded()` before reading `fittingSize` to ensure Auto Layout has resolved the new intrinsic content sizes."

### 6. Specify initial value of `currentDisplayMode` (Step 2, completeness)

**Proposed edit**: In Step 2, add:
- "Initialize `currentDisplayMode` to `.regular` in the property declaration. This matches the initial constraint setup (all constraints active, full zoom text)."

## Risks & mitigations

- **Risk**: `layout()` override with constraint changes causing performance issues during continuous pinch zoom (layout is called at display refresh rate during magnification changes). **Mitigation**: The mode-equality guard (`guard newMode != currentDisplayMode`) means constraint changes only happen at mode transitions, not every frame. The width check and mode computation are O(1). Acceptable.

- **Risk**: The `fittingSize` target of `< 300` might still be exceeded if indexLabel + sizeLabel content is very long (e.g., "999 / 9999" + "40000 x 30000"). **Mitigation**: The plan's constraint strategy uses `>=4` spacing between sizeLabel and indexLabel, and both labels have default compression resistance. With `.defaultLow` on zoomLabel, fittingSize is dominated by indexLabel + sizeLabel, which even in worst case ("999 / 9999" + "40000 x 30000" at 11pt system font) should total roughly 200-220pt with padding. Well under 300.

- **Risk**: `NSVisualEffectView` subclass overriding `layout()` — NSVisualEffectView has its own layout behavior for the material effect. **Mitigation**: Calling `super.layout()` first is the standard pattern and preserves the visual effect compositing. Other views in the codebase (ImageScrollView, EmptyStateView) already use this pattern successfully.

## Test plan

- **Update existing**: `testStatusBarManualZoomTextExpandsFittingWidth` — change `XCTAssertGreaterThan(statusBar.fittingSize.width, 500)` to `XCTAssertLessThan(statusBar.fittingSize.width, 350)`. Add `statusBar.layoutSubtreeIfNeeded()` before assertion.
- **Update existing**: `testEffectiveMinimumContentSize_prefersLayoutFittingWidthWhenLarger` — change `layoutMinContentSize` from `NSSize(width: 568, height: 22)` to a value below 300 (e.g., `NSSize(width: 250, height: 22)`) and assert `effective.width == 300` (now clamped by `Constants.minWindowContentWidth`).
- **New**: `testZoomStatusFormatter_compactPercentOnly_manual` — verify `ZoomStatusFormatter.text(for: .manual(percent: 89, windowAuto: true), style: .compactPercentOnly)` returns `"89%"`.
- **New**: `testZoomStatusFormatter_compactPercentOnly_fit` — verify `.fit` with `.compactPercentOnly` still returns `"FIT"`.
- **New**: `testZoomStatusFormatter_compactPercentOnly_actual` — verify `.actual(windowAuto: true)` with `.compactPercentOnly` returns `"100%"`.
- **New**: `testStatusBarDisplayMode_regularAtWideWidth` — create StatusBarView with wide frame (~600pt), call `update(...)` with full zoom text, verify zoomLabel is visible with full text.
- **New**: `testStatusBarDisplayMode_compactAtNarrowWidth` — create StatusBarView with narrow frame (~350pt), trigger layout, verify zoom text is compact format.
- **New**: `testStatusBarDisplayMode_minimalAtVeryNarrowWidth` — create StatusBarView with very narrow frame (~250pt), trigger layout, verify zoomLabel is hidden and its constraints are deactivated.
- **New**: `testStatusBarDisplayMode_hysteresis` — set frame to compact threshold width, verify compact mode. Then increase by 10pt (below hysteresis), verify still compact. Increase by 20pt more, verify switches to regular.
- **Build verification**: `xcodebuild build` + `xcodebuild build -target CeeTests` to ensure clean compilation.

## Open questions

1. The plan's proposed layout is `[leading]-8-[sizeLabel]->=4-[spacer]-[indexLabel]-8-[zoomLabel]-8-[trailing]`. What is `[spacer]` — an invisible NSView, or a `>=` constraint between sizeLabel and indexLabel? If the latter, the layout is effectively `[leading]-8-[sizeLabel]->=4-[indexLabel]-8-[zoomLabel]-8-[trailing]` with no explicit spacer view needed. Please clarify whether an actual spacer view is intended, as it adds unnecessary complexity versus a simple `>=` constraint.

2. Currently `indexLabel` has no leading/trailing positional constraint relative to the container — it floats based on `trailingAnchor == zoomLabel.leadingAnchor - 12`. In the new layout, when zoomLabel is deactivated (minimal mode), indexLabel needs an alternate trailing constraint. The plan mentions deactivating zoomLabel constraints, but should indexLabel in minimal mode be right-aligned (trailing to container), left-aligned (after sizeLabel), or centered? This affects the visual balance.

Review the UPDATED implementation plan at /Users/pc035860/code/cee/.factory/docs/2026-03-14-narrow-window-friendly-status-bar.md

This is a re-review after applying patches from the first review. The plan was updated to address:
1. Specific compression resistance values and constraint strategy for fittingSize reduction
2. Explicit lifecycle hook (layout()) with infinite loop guard for display mode switching
3. Hysteresis for display mode transitions
4. Deactivating zoomLabel constraints in minimal mode (CLAUDE.md fittingSize gotcha)
5. ZoomStatusStyle enum for formatter
6. State management for ZoomStatusMode in StatusBarView
7. Updated test plan with existing test updates
8. fittingSize target < 300

Context: macOS image viewer app (AppKit, Swift 6.2), called "Cee". Key CLAUDE.md gotchas:
- Hidden views still affect fittingSize — isHidden=true does NOT deactivate Auto Layout constraints
- setMagnification synchronously triggers reflectScrolledClipView

Review for correctness, feasibility, completeness, and risk. Focus on whether the previous critical issues are now adequately addressed.

Lowering StatusBarView's fittingSize will directly reduce this value.

### Interface/Contract Check
- `StatusBarView.update(index:total:zoomMode:imageSize:indexOverride:)` is called from `ImageViewController` lines 276, 294, 302. The plan preserves this API, only adding internal state storage for `zoomMode`.
- `StatusBarView.updateZoom(_:)` is called at line 327. The plan preserves this API.
- `ZoomStatusFormatter.text(for:)` is a static method — adding a `style:` parameter with a default value of `.full` maintains backward compatibility with existing callers.

---

## Verdict

**PASS_WITH_CHANGES**

## Critical issues (must fix)

None. The first review's critical issues have been adequately addressed.

## Plan patch (proposed edits)

### 1. Clarify constraint deactivation scope in minimal mode (Step 2, clarification)

Step 2 says "deactivate `zoomLabel` 的水平約束" in minimal mode. This is correct in principle, but the implementation needs to be precise: the constraints to deactivate are zoomLabel's **leading/trailing** anchors (positional constraints), not its intrinsic content size constraints. Additionally, `indexLabel.trailingAnchor == zoomLabel.leadingAnchor - 12` (the current inter-label spacing constraint) must also be deactivated or replaced, otherwise deactivating zoomLabel's trailing anchor alone creates an ambiguous layout. 

**Proposed edit**: In Step 2, add a bullet:
- "When entering minimal mode, deactivate both the zoomLabel trailing anchor and the indexLabel-to-zoomLabel spacing constraint. Replace indexLabel's trailing constraint with a direct `indexLabel.trailingAnchor <= trailingAnchor - 8`. Store these as separate constraint groups (e.g., `zoomLabelConstraints` vs `minimalModeIndexTrailingConstraint`) for clean activate/deactivate."

### 2. Add default parameter to ZoomStatusFormatter.text (Step 3, minor)

**Proposed edit**: In Step 3, explicitly note that the new `style` parameter should have a default value of `.full`:
- `static func text(for mode: ZoomStatusMode, style: ZoomStatusStyle = .full) -> String`

This ensures all 4 existing call sites (`ImageViewControllerTests` x3 + `StatusBarView.updateZoom`) need zero changes.

### 3. Clarify hysteresis direction semantics (Step 2, clarification)

The plan says "從 regular->compact 的閾值比 compact->regular 小 20pt." This phrasing is ambiguous — "smaller threshold" could mean the width threshold is 20pt lower (which is correct: you need to shrink further to trigger compaction) or 20pt less (which is the same thing, but reads oddly). 

**Proposed edit**: Rephrase to: "Hysteresis: `regular->compact` triggers at width W, `compact->regular` triggers at width W+20pt. This means the view must grow 20pt wider than the compact threshold before it expands back to regular."

### 4. Consider `needsLayout` guard instead of pure mode-equality guard (Step 2, robustness)

The plan uses `guard newMode != currentDisplayMode else { return }` as the infinite loop guard. This is sufficient for preventing oscillation since the mode is stored and compared. However, there is a subtle risk: if constraint activation/deactivation in `applyDisplayMode()` causes a layout pass that re-enters `layout()`, the mode will already be set, so the guard catches it. This is correct. No change needed, but worth noting explicitly that `applyDisplayMode()` must set `currentDisplayMode = newMode` **before** activating/deactivating constraints.

**Proposed edit**: In Step 2, add:
- "In `applyDisplayMode()`, set `currentDisplayMode = newMode` BEFORE activating/deactivating constraints, since constraint changes trigger synchronous layout passes that re-enter `layout()`."

### 5. Add note about fittingSize measurement timing in tests (Step 7)

**Proposed edit**: In Step 7, add:
- "After calling `update(...)` on the test StatusBarView, call `layoutSubtreeIfNeeded()` before reading `fittingSize` to ensure Auto Layout has resolved the new intrinsic content sizes."

### 6. Specify initial value of `currentDisplayMode` (Step 2, completeness)

**Proposed edit**: In Step 2, add:
- "Initialize `currentDisplayMode` to `.regular` in the property declaration. This matches the initial constraint setup (all constraints active, full zoom text)."

## Risks & mitigations

- **Risk**: `layout()` override with constraint changes causing performance issues during continuous pinch zoom (layout is called at display refresh rate during magnification changes). **Mitigation**: The mode-equality guard (`guard newMode != currentDisplayMode`) means constraint changes only happen at mode transitions, not every frame. The width check and mode computation are O(1). Acceptable.

- **Risk**: The `fittingSize` target of `< 300` might still be exceeded if indexLabel + sizeLabel content is very long (e.g., "999 / 9999" + "40000 x 30000"). **Mitigation**: The plan's constraint strategy uses `>=4` spacing between sizeLabel and indexLabel, and both labels have default compression resistance. With `.defaultLow` on zoomLabel, fittingSize is dominated by indexLabel + sizeLabel, which even in worst case ("999 / 9999" + "40000 x 30000" at 11pt system font) should total roughly 200-220pt with padding. Well under 300.

- **Risk**: `NSVisualEffectView` subclass overriding `layout()` — NSVisualEffectView has its own layout behavior for the material effect. **Mitigation**: Calling `super.layout()` first is the standard pattern and preserves the visual effect compositing. Other views in the codebase (ImageScrollView, EmptyStateView) already use this pattern successfully.

## Test plan

- **Update existing**: `testStatusBarManualZoomTextExpandsFittingWidth` — change `XCTAssertGreaterThan(statusBar.fittingSize.width, 500)` to `XCTAssertLessThan(statusBar.fittingSize.width, 350)`. Add `statusBar.layoutSubtreeIfNeeded()` before assertion.
- **Update existing**: `testEffectiveMinimumContentSize_prefersLayoutFittingWidthWhenLarger` — change `layoutMinContentSize` from `NSSize(width: 568, height: 22)` to a value below 300 (e.g., `NSSize(width: 250, height: 22)`) and assert `effective.width == 300` (now clamped by `Constants.minWindowContentWidth`).
- **New**: `testZoomStatusFormatter_compactPercentOnly_manual` — verify `ZoomStatusFormatter.text(for: .manual(percent: 89, windowAuto: true), style: .compactPercentOnly)` returns `"89%"`.
- **New**: `testZoomStatusFormatter_compactPercentOnly_fit` — verify `.fit` with `.compactPercentOnly` still returns `"FIT"`.
- **New**: `testZoomStatusFormatter_compactPercentOnly_actual` — verify `.actual(windowAuto: true)` with `.compactPercentOnly` returns `"100%"`.
- **New**: `testStatusBarDisplayMode_regularAtWideWidth` — create StatusBarView with wide frame (~600pt), call `update(...)` with full zoom text, verify zoomLabel is visible with full text.
- **New**: `testStatusBarDisplayMode_compactAtNarrowWidth` — create StatusBarView with narrow frame (~350pt), trigger layout, verify zoom text is compact format.
- **New**: `testStatusBarDisplayMode_minimalAtVeryNarrowWidth` — create StatusBarView with very narrow frame (~250pt), trigger layout, verify zoomLabel is hidden and its constraints are deactivated.
- **New**: `testStatusBarDisplayMode_hysteresis` — set frame to compact threshold width, verify compact mode. Then increase by 10pt (below hysteresis), verify still compact. Increase by 20pt more, verify switches to regular.
- **Build verification**: `xcodebuild build` + `xcodebuild build -target CeeTests` to ensure clean compilation.

## Open questions

1. The plan's proposed layout is `[leading]-8-[sizeLabel]->=4-[spacer]-[indexLabel]-8-[zoomLabel]-8-[trailing]`. What is `[spacer]` — an invisible NSView, or a `>=` constraint between sizeLabel and indexLabel? If the latter, the layout is effectively `[leading]-8-[sizeLabel]->=4-[indexLabel]-8-[zoomLabel]-8-[trailing]` with no explicit spacer view needed. Please clarify whether an actual spacer view is intended, as it adds unnecessary complexity versus a simple `>=` constraint.

2. Currently `indexLabel` has no leading/trailing positional constraint relative to the container — it floats based on `trailingAnchor == zoomLabel.leadingAnchor - 12`. In the new layout, when zoomLabel is deactivated (minimal mode), indexLabel needs an alternate trailing constraint. The plan mentions deactivating zoomLabel constraints, but should indexLabel in minimal mode be right-aligned (trailing to container), left-aligned (after sizeLabel), or centered? This affects the visual balance.
嗯呢，第二輪 review：**無 critical issues**，剩下的都是實作細節澄清。已經夠清楚了~

1→import AppKit
     2→
     3→enum ZoomStatusMode: Equatable {
     4→    case fit
     5→    case actual(windowAuto: Bool)
     6→    case manual(percent: Int, windowAuto: Bool)
     7→}
     8→
     9→enum ZoomStatusFormatter {
    10→    static func text(for mode: ZoomStatusMode) -> String {
    11→        let baseText: String
    12→        let windowAuto: Bool
    13→
    14→        switch mode {
    15→        case .fit:
    16→            return String(localized: "status.fit")
    17→        case .actual(let isWindowAuto):
    18→            baseText = String(localized: "status.actual") + " 100%"
    19→            windowAuto = isWindowAuto
    20→        case .manual(let percent, let isWindowAuto):
    21→            baseText = String(localized: "status.manual") + " \(percent)%"
    22→            windowAuto = isWindowAuto
    23→        }
    24→
    25→        guard windowAuto else { return baseText }
    26→        return baseText + " · " + String(localized: "status.windowAuto")
    27→    }
    28→}
    29→
    30→final class StatusBarView: NSVisualEffectView {
    31→
    32→    // MARK: - UI Elements
    33→
    34→    private let separator = NSBox()
    35→    private let sizeLabel = NSTextField(labelWithString: "")
    36→    private let indexLabel = NSTextField(labelWithString: "")
    37→    private let zoomLabel = NSTextField(labelWithString: "")
    38→
    39→    // MARK: - Initialization
    40→
    41→    override init(frame frameRect: NSRect) {
    42→        super.init(frame: frameRect)
    43→        setupVisualEffect()
    44→        setupUI()
    45→    }
    46→
    47→    required init?(coder: NSCoder) {
    48→        super.init(coder: coder)
    49→        setupVisualEffect()
    50→        setupUI()
    51→    }
    52→
    53→    private func setupVisualEffect() {
    54→        // 設定毛玻璃效果，與標題列一致
    55→        material = .titlebar
    56→        blendingMode = .withinWindow
    57→        state = .active
    58→    }
    59→
    60→    private func setupUI() {
    61→        // 頂部分隔線（NSBox 原生支援 dynamic color，深淺模式自動切換）
    62→        separator.boxType = .separator
    63→
    64→        // 設定文字樣式
    65→        [sizeLabel, indexLabel, zoomLabel].forEach { label in
    66→            label.font = .systemFont(ofSize: 11)
    67→            label.textColor = NSColor.secondaryLabelColor
    68→            label.alignment = .center
    69→            label.lineBreakMode = .byClipping
    70→        }
    71→
    72→        // 加入 subviews
    73→        addSubview(separator)
    74→        addSubview(sizeLabel)
    75→        addSubview(indexLabel)
    76→        addSubview(zoomLabel)
    77→
    78→        // Auto Layout
    79→        separator.translatesAutoresizingMaskIntoConstraints = false
    80→        sizeLabel.translatesAutoresizingMaskIntoConstraints = false
    81→        indexLabel.translatesAutoresizingMaskIntoConstraints = false
    82→        zoomLabel.translatesAutoresizingMaskIntoConstraints = false
    83→
    84→        // zoomLabel 靠右側（固定最小寬度，不截斷）
    85→        zoomLabel.setContentCompressionResistancePriority(.required, for: .horizontal)
    86→
    87→        NSLayoutConstraint.activate([
    88→            // 分隔線：頂部全寬
    89→            separator.topAnchor.constraint(equalTo: topAnchor),
    90→            separator.leadingAnchor.constraint(equalTo: leadingAnchor),
    91→            separator.trailingAnchor.constraint(equalTo: trailingAnchor),
    92→
    93→            // 尺寸標籤（置中）
    94→            sizeLabel.centerYAnchor.constraint(equalTo: centerYAnchor),
    95→            sizeLabel.centerXAnchor.constraint(equalTo: centerXAnchor),
    96→
    97→            // 縮放標籤（最右側）
    98→            zoomLabel.centerYAnchor.constraint(equalTo: centerYAnchor),
    99→            zoomLabel.trailingAnchor.constraint(equalTo: trailingAnchor, constant: -8),
   100→            zoomLabel.widthAnchor.constraint(greaterThanOrEqualToConstant: 36),
   101→
   102→            // 索引標籤（zoomLabel 左側，保持間距）
   103→            indexLabel.centerYAnchor.constraint(equalTo: centerYAnchor),
   104→            indexLabel.trailingAnchor.constraint(equalTo: zoomLabel.leadingAnchor, constant: -12),
   105→
   106→            // sizeLabel 不能超出 indexLabel 左邊
   107→            sizeLabel.trailingAnchor.constraint(lessThanOrEqualTo: indexLabel.leadingAnchor, constant: -8),
   108→        ])
   109→    }
   110→
   111→    // MARK: - Update Methods
   112→
   113→    func update(index: Int, total: Int, zoomMode: ZoomStatusMode, imageSize: NSSize,
   114→                indexOverride: String? = nil) {
   115→        indexLabel.stringValue = indexOverride ?? "\(index) / \(total)"
   116→        zoomLabel.stringValue = ZoomStatusFormatter.text(for: zoomMode)
   117→        sizeLabel.stringValue = "\(Int(imageSize.width)) × \(Int(imageSize.height))"
   118→    }
   119→
   120→    func updateZoom(_ zoomMode: ZoomStatusMode) {
   121→        zoomLabel.stringValue = ZoomStatusFormatter.text(for: zoomMode)
   122→    }
   123→
   124→    /// 僅更新索引
   125→    func updateIndex(current: Int, total: Int) {
   126→        indexLabel.stringValue = "\(current) / \(total)"
   127→    }
   128→
   129→    /// 清空所有顯示內容（用於 empty state）
   130→    func clear() {
   131→        sizeLabel.stringValue = ""
   132→        indexLabel.stringValue = ""
   133→        zoomLabel.stringValue = ""
   134→    }
   135→}

1→import XCTest
     2→@testable import Cee
     3→
     4→@MainActor
     5→final class ImageViewControllerTests: XCTestCase {
     6→
     7→    func testZoomStatusFormatter_fit() {
     8→        XCTAssertEqual(ZoomStatusFormatter.text(for: .fit), String(localized: "status.fit"))
     9→    }
    10→
    11→    func testZoomStatusFormatter_actualWithWindowAuto() {
    12→        XCTAssertEqual(
    13→            ZoomStatusFormatter.text(for: .actual(windowAuto: true)),
    14→            String(localized: "status.actual") + " 100% · " + String(localized: "status.windowAuto")
    15→        )
    16→    }
    17→
    18→    func testZoomStatusFormatter_manualWithWindowAuto() {
    19→        XCTAssertEqual(
    20→            ZoomStatusFormatter.text(for: .manual(percent: 125, windowAuto: true)),
    21→            String(localized: "status.manual") + " 125% · " + String(localized: "status.windowAuto")
    22→        )
    23→    }
    24→
    25→    func testStatusBarManualZoomTextExpandsFittingWidth() {
    26→        let statusBar = StatusBarView(frame: NSRect(x: 0, y: 0, width: 10, height: Constants.statusBarHeight))
    27→
    28→        statusBar.update(
    29→            index: 1,
    30→            total: 999,
    31→            zoomMode: .manual(percent: 100, windowAuto: true),
    32→            imageSize: NSSize(width: 4000, height: 3000)
    33→        )
    34→
    35→        XCTAssertGreaterThan(statusBar.fittingSize.width, 500)
    36→    }
    37→
    38→    func testScrollViewEffectiveMinMagnificationUsesDocumentBaseSize() {
    39→        let scrollView = ImageScrollView(frame: NSRect(x: 0, y: 0, width: 800, height: 600))
    40→        scrollView.documentView = NSView(frame: NSRect(x: 0, y: 0, width: 1000, height: 500))
    41→
    42→        XCTAssertEqual(scrollView.effectiveMinMagnification(), 0.6, accuracy: 0.001)
    43→    }
    44→
    45→    private var controller: ImageViewController!
    46→    private var scrollView: ImageScrollView!
    47→    private var documentView: DualPageContentView!
    48→
    49→    private func makeController(
    50→        contentSize: NSSize = NSSize(width: 1000, height: 800),
    51→        imageSize: NSSize = NSSize(width: 800, height: 400)
    52→    ) throws {
    53→        controller = ImageViewController()
    54→        controller.loadViewIfNeeded()
    55→        controller.settings.showStatusBar = false
    56→        controller.settings.alwaysFitOnOpen = true
    57→        controller.settings.isManualZoom = false
    58→        controller.settings.fittingOptions = FittingOptions(
    59→            shrinkHorizontally: false,
    60→            shrinkVertically: false,
    61→            stretchHorizontally: true,
    62→            stretchVertically: false
    63→        )
    64→
    65→        controller.view.frame = NSRect(origin: .zero, size: contentSize)
    66→        controller.view.layoutSubtreeIfNeeded()
    67→
    68→        scrollView = try XCTUnwrap(
    69→            controller.view.subviews.first(where: { $0 is ImageScrollView }) as? ImageScrollView
    70→        )
    71→        documentView = try XCTUnwrap(scrollView.documentView as? DualPageContentView)
    72→        documentView.configureSingle(imageSize: imageSize)
    73→        controller.view.layoutSubtreeIfNeeded()
    74→    }
    75→
    76→    func testHandleWindowDidResize_reappliesAutoFitMagnification() throws {
    77→        try makeController()
    78→
    79→        controller.handleWindowDidResize()
    80→        XCTAssertEqual(scrollView.magnification, 1.25, accuracy: 0.001)
    81→
    82→        controller.view.frame = NSRect(x: 0, y: 0, width: 1200, height: 800)
    83→        controller.view.layoutSubtreeIfNeeded()
    84→
    85→        controller.handleWindowDidResize()
    86→        XCTAssertEqual(scrollView.magnification, 1.5, accuracy: 0.001)
    87→    }
    88→
    89→    func testHandleWindowDidResize_preservesManualZoom() throws {
    90→        try makeController()
    91→
    92→        controller.handleWindowDidResize()
    93→        let initialMagnification = scrollView.magnification
    94→        controller.settings.isManualZoom = true

content.text:
Execute git commit with these ABSOLUTE CONSTRAINTS:

CONSTRAINTS:
1. NO Claude/Anthropic/AI attribution in commit message
2. PRIORITY: If project's CLAUDE.md exists and specifies commit style, use that style FIRST
   - Always check for project-specific commit conventions in CLAUDE.md
   - Project's commit style takes precedence over default Conventional Commits

COMMIT MESSAGE FORMAT:
- Default Pattern: type(scope): description
- Types: feat, fix, refactor, chore, docs, style, test, remove
- Language: English
- Examples:
  - feat(search): add new search feature
  - fix(cart): resolve checkout error
  - docs(readme): update installation guide

AUTO MODE:
- If `--auto` flag is present in <additional_instructions>, enable auto mode
- In auto mode: skip user selection (step 6), automatically choose the best commit message and execute immediately
- Strip `--auto` from the additional instructions before using them as supplementary context

GIT FLAGS PASSTHROUGH:
- If `--no-gpg-sign` flag is present in <additional_instructions>, append `--no-gpg-sign` to the git commit command
- If `--no-verify` flag is present in <additional_instructions>, append `--no-verify` to the git commit command
- Strip these flags from the additional instructions before using them as supplementary context

PROCESS:
1. Check: Look for CLAUDE.md in project root for commit style specifications
2. Check: git status
3. Verify: only "Changes to be committed" will be included
4. Review: git log to understand project's commit style
5. Generate: Create 4 different commit message options based on the changes
6. Present (skip in auto mode): Use AskUserQuestion to show only the commit heading (first line) for each option
7. Commit: Execute git commit with the selected message (full message with body if applicable)

COMMIT MESSAGE OPTIONS:
- Generate 4 different commit messages with varying:
  - Commit types (feat, fix, refactor, chore, docs, style, etc.)
  - Scope specificity (broader vs. narrower scope)
  - Description detail level (concise vs. detailed)
- Each option should accurately reflect the staged changes
- Only display the heading (first line, e.g., "feat(auth): add JWT token validation") in AskUserQuestion
- Keep the full commit message (with body if needed) ready for execution after selection

AUTO-SELECTION CRITERIA (when --auto is active):
- Choose the most accurate commit type reflecting the primary change
- Use specific scope when changes are focused on one area
- Use broader scope or omit when changes span multiple areas
- Prefer concise, descriptive messages
- Include body only for complex changes requiring explanation

Violation of these constraints should result in immediate task termination.

<additional_instructions>

</additional_instructions>

Replace center-anchored sizeLabel layout with left-aligned compressible
horizontal layout. StatusBarView now switches between three display modes
(regular/compact/minimal) based on available width with 20pt hysteresis.

- Remove sizeLabel.centerXAnchor constraint that inflated fittingSize to 500+px
- Lower zoomLabel compression resistance and positioning constraints to
  .defaultHigh so fittingSize ignores zoom label width
- Add ZoomStatusStyle enum with full/compactPercentOnly formatting
- Deactivate zoomLabel constraints in minimal mode (fittingSize gotcha)
- Store ZoomStatusMode for re-formatting on display mode transitions
EOF
)"

content.text:
Base directory for this skill: /Users/pc035860/.claude/skills/review-loop

# Review Loop

## Overview

This skill enables continuous, automated code review and improvement cycles using Codex MCP, Cursor CLI, Gemini CLI, or code-auditor subagent. The loop automatically:
- Performs initial code/spec review
- Identifies blocking issues, suggestions, and decision points
- Fixes blocking issues that don't require user decisions
- Continues reviewing until clean or requires user input

**Core Features**: Automatic looping, cross-session support, intelligent interruption, progress tracking

**Script Location**: `scripts/` is in the same directory as this SKILL.md file. Derive the absolute path from this file's location when executing.

## Tool Selection

Choose your tool and refer to the corresponding implementation guide:

### Codex MCP
**Use when**: Working within Claude Code with MCP support, need session history, native integration

**Implementation**: Read `references/codex-implementation.md` for detailed steps

### Cursor CLI
**Use when**: Working with Cursor editor, need gpt-5.4 model, prefer CLI workflows

**Implementation**: Read `references/cursor-implementation.md` for detailed steps

### Gemini CLI
**Use when**: Standalone Gemini CLI access, need gemini-3.1-pro model, prefer lightweight CLI workflows

**Implementation**: Read `references/gemini-implementation.md` for detailed steps

### Subagent (code-auditor)
**Use when**: Claude Code with Agent tool available, prefer native subagent over external tools, no Codex/Cursor/Gemini setup

**Implementation**: Read `references/subagent-implementation.md` for detailed steps

**Fallback**: If `code-auditor` subagent is not available, use `generalPurpose` (or any general subagent) instead.

---

All four tools share the same evaluation logic and termination conditions described below.

## Common Loop Workflow

All tools follow the same review cycle:

```
Initial Review
  ↓
Save Session ID (via TodoWrite): conversation ID / chat ID / agentId
  ↓
Evaluate Results ──→ No blocking? ──Yes→ Complete ✓
  ↓ No
Need decision? ──Yes→ [Context Gathering Flow]*
  ↓ No                      ↓
Fix Issues              Recommend
  ↓                         ↓
Continue Review ──┐     Ask User
  ↓               │         ↓
Evaluate Results ─┘     Pause ⏸️

* See references/context-gathering-guide.md
```

## Evaluation Logic

After each review, evaluate results using this decision tree:

### Situation A - No Blocking Issues
**Action**: End loop ✓
- All critical issues resolved
- Code meets quality standards

### Situation B - Requires User Decision
**Action**: Context Check → Explore (if needed) → Recommend → Pause ⏸️

Before asking the user, ensure sufficient context:
1. Check if context is sufficient to provide informed recommendations
2. If insufficient, gather context using Explore agent
3. Provide recommendation with collected context
4. Then ask user for decision

**Implementation**: Read `references/context-gathering-guide.md` for detailed steps

**When to trigger**:
- Architecture design choices
- Feature scope definition
- Technology stack selection
- Business logic decisions
- Security trade-offs

### Situation C - Auto-Fixable Issues
**Action**: Continue to fix issues
**When**:
- Syntax/type errors
- Logic errors
- Style violations
- Performance optimizations
- Naming conventions

Fix only clear, non-decision issues, then continue review cycle.

## Termination Conditions

### Complete Termination ✓
- No blocking issues remain
- Code meets quality standards
- All auto-fixable issues resolved

### Pause Termination ⏸️
- User decision required
- Architecture/feature/technology choices needed
- Cannot proceed without business logic input

## Quick Reference

### Scripts Available
- **Codex**: `scripts/get-codex-conversation-id.sh` - Retrieves conversation ID from session history
- **Cursor**: `scripts/cursor-review.sh` - Wrapper for cursor-agent review commands
- **Gemini**: `scripts/gemini-review.sh` - Wrapper for gemini CLI review commands
- **Subagent**: Agent tool with `subagent_type=code-auditor` (fallback: `generalPurpose`), save `agentId` for resume

### Must Pause For
- Architecture decisions
- Feature scope
- Technology choices
- Business logic
- Security trade-offs

### Can Auto-Handle
- Syntax errors
- Type errors
- Style issues
- Simple optimizations
- Naming conventions

### Self-Check Before Loop
- [ ] Defined review completion criteria?
- [ ] Clarified pause decision points?
- [ ] Prepared project path?
- [ ] Selected appropriate tool (Codex/Cursor/Gemini/Subagent)?

### Self-Check During Evaluation
- [ ] Any blocking issues?
- [ ] Requires user decision?
- [ ] Can auto-fix?

### Self-Check Before Loop End
- [ ] All blocking issues fixed?
- [ ] Session ID saved (first time)? (Codex: conversation ID, Cursor: chat ID, Subagent: agentId)
- [ ] Fixes documented?

file.content:
# Codex Implementation Guide

This guide provides detailed steps for using the Review Loop with Codex MCP.

## Prerequisites

- Claude Code environment with MCP support
- Codex MCP server configured
- Access to `mcp__codex__codex` and `mcp__codex__codex-reply` tools

## Step 1: Initial Review

**IMPORTANT**: Set MCP tool timeout to maximum value as execution takes time.

**⚠️ MODEL**: Always use `model: "gpt-5.4"` — do NOT change the model parameter. This is the latest and most capable model available.

**⚠️ SANDBOX**: Always use `sandbox: "workspace-write"`. Using `"read-only"` will cause Codex to hang when it attempts to run test commands that require write access.

Choose review prompt based on file type:

### For Spec/Plan Files

Use this prompt when reviewing files with names containing `spec`, `plan`, or `design`:

```typescript
mcp__codex__codex({
  prompt: `Review this spec/plan thoroughly.

## Output Contract
Respond in exactly this section order:
1. **Blocking Issues** (must fix) — format: \`[file:line]\` issue — impact
2. **Suggestions** (non-blocking improvements) — format: \`[file:line]\` suggestion — rationale
3. **Questions** (require user decision) — format: question — why decision is needed
4. **Summary** (1-2 sentences on overall readiness)

## Review Focus
1. Completeness: Are all relevant files listed? How do parts collaborate/integrate?
2. Feasibility: Are implementation steps clear and executable? Are dependencies explicit?
3. Coherence: Is the path from current state to goal state complete?
4. MVP Principles: Any over-engineering? Is the core function clearly scoped?

## Execution Rules
- Read ALL relevant files before reporting; maintain an internal checklist of files to review
- Base every issue on retrieved file content only — never fabricate problems
- Cite exact file:line for each finding; mark blocked items with what information is missing
- Continue reading files until the review is complete; retry with different search strategies if results are empty
- Verify each issue before reporting: does the problem actually exist at that location?
- Do NOT spawn subagents or trigger additional review rounds — complete your entire review in a single response`,
  cwd: "/path/to/project",
  model: "gpt-5.4",
  profile: "mcp",
  sandbox: "workspace-write"
})
```

### For Code Files

Use this prompt when reviewing code files:

```typescript
mcp__codex__codex({
  prompt: `Review [describe content] thoroughly.

## Output Contract
Respond in exactly this section order:
1. **Blocking Issues** (must fix) — format: \`[file:line]\` issue — impact
2. **Suggestions** (non-blocking improvements) — format: \`[file:line]\` suggestion — rationale
3. **Questions** (require user decision) — format: question — why decision is needed
4. **Summary** (1-2 sentences on overall code quality)

## Review Focus
1. Code quality and maintainability
2. Potential bugs or logic errors
3. Performance and security considerations

## Execution Rules
- Read ALL relevant files before reporting; maintain an internal checklist
- Base every finding on retrieved code only — never fabricate issues
- Cite exact file:line for each finding; mark blocked items with what information is missing
- Continue reading files until the review is complete; retry with different strategies if results are empty
- Verify each issue: confirm the problem exists at the cited location before reporting
- Do NOT spawn subagents or trigger additional review rounds — complete your entire review in a single response`,
  cwd: "/path/to/project",
  model: "gpt-5.4",
  profile: "mcp",
  sandbox: "workspace-write"
})
```

## Step 2: Retrieve Conversation ID

After the initial review completes, retrieve the conversation ID for future reference:

```bash
# Execute script to get matching Conversation ID
CONVERSATION_ID=$(scripts/get-codex-conversation-id.sh)
```

**IMPORTANT**: Save `CONVERSATION_ID` in todo list for later use:

```typescript
TodoWrite({
  todos: [
    {
      content: "Codex Conversation ID: ${CONVERSATION_ID}",
      status: "pending",
      activeForm: "Tracking Codex review session"
    }
  ]
})
```

### How the Script Works

The `get-codex-conversation-id.sh` script automatically:
- Searches today's session files in `~/.codex/sessions/YYYY/MM/DD/`
- Matches by current working directory
- Falls back to latest session if no CWD match found

### Manual Retrieval

If the script fails, you can manually find the conversation ID in:
```
~/.codex/sessions/YYYY/MM/DD/*.jsonl
```

Look for `session_meta` entries with matching `cwd`.

## Step 3: Continue Review (After Fixes)

⚠️ **CRITICAL**: Always use `mcp__codex__codex-reply` to continue the review session. Never use `mcp__codex__codex` which would start a new session.

**Exception**: Only use `mcp__codex__codex` (non-reply version) if the user **explicitly requests** to start a new session.

After fixing blocking issues, continue the review cycle:

```typescript
mcp__codex__codex-reply({
  conversationId: "[ID from Todo]",
  prompt: `Fixed: [list fixes]. Please continue review.

## Output Contract
Same as before — respond in order: Blocking Issues → Suggestions → Questions → Summary.
Verify all previous blocking issues are resolved before reporting new ones.
Do NOT spawn subagents or trigger additional review rounds — complete your entire review in a single response.`
})
```

### Example Fix Description

```typescript
mcp__codex__codex-reply({
  conversationId: "abc123-session-id",
  prompt: `Fixed:
1. Corrected type error in auth.ts
2. Added missing error handling
3. Optimized database query performance

Please continue review. Use same output contract: Blocking Issues → Suggestions → Questions → Summary.
Verify the above fixes are actually applied before proceeding.
Do NOT spawn subagents or trigger additional review rounds — complete your entire review in a single response.`
})
```

## Important Notes

### Timeout Configuration

Codex MCP tools require extended execution time. Always:
- Set MCP tool timeout to **maximum value** (600000ms recommended)
- Be patient during execution - review can take several minutes

### Conversation Persistence

- Conversations are stored in `~/.codex/sessions/`
- Sessions persist across Claude Code restarts
- You can resume reviews from previous days if needed

### uv Test 設定

如果要跑 uv test，必須在 Prompt 中提示設定 uv 的 cache 環境變數到 TMP 路徑底下，否則會因 Sandbox 權限問題而卡住：

- **`UV_CACHE_DIR`**：套件 cache（等同 `--cache-dir`），預設為 `$HOME/.cache/uv`
- **`UV_PYTHON_CACHE_DIR`**（若用到 managed Python）：Python 安裝 archive 的 cache

範例（在 Prompt 中指示 Codex 執行時）：
```bash
UV_CACHE_DIR="${TMPDIR:-/tmp}/uv-cache" UV_PYTHON_CACHE_DIR="${TMPDIR:-/tmp}/uv-python-cache" uv run pytest
```

### Error Handling

If the initial review fails:
1. Check MCP server connection
2. Verify Codex profile configuration
3. Ensure correct working directory
4. Review timeout settings

## Next Steps

After completing Step 3, return to the **Evaluation Logic** section in the main SKILL.md to determine:
- Whether to end the loop (no blocking issues)
- Whether to pause (requires user decision)
- Whether to continue (more auto-fixable issues)

Review the recent changes on branch feat/narrow-window-friendly-status-bar thoroughly. Focus on the diff from the latest commit.

The changes refactor StatusBarView to be narrow-window friendly:
1. Replaced center-anchored layout with compressible horizontal layout
2. Added DisplayMode enum (regular/compact/minimal) with hysteresis
3. Extended ZoomStatusFormatter with ZoomStatusStyle (full/compactPercentOnly)
4. Lowered zoomLabel compression resistance and positioning constraint priorities

Key files changed:
- Cee/Views/StatusBarView.swift (main implementation)
- CeeTests/ImageViewControllerTests.swift (updated + new tests)
- CeeTests/ImageWindowControllerTests.swift (new test)

## Output Contract
Respond in exactly this section order:
1. **Blocking Issues** (must fix) — format: `[file:line]` issue — impact
2. **Suggestions** (non-blocking improvements) — format: `[file:line]` suggestion — rationale
3. **Questions** (require user decision) — format: question — why decision is needed
4. **Summary** (1-2 sentences on overall code quality)

## Review Focus
1. Code quality and maintainability
2. Potential bugs or logic errors (especially Auto Layout constraint conflicts, infinite layout loops, fittingSize behavior)
3. Performance considerations (layout() override frequency during pinch zoom)
4. Test coverage adequacy

## Execution Rules
- Read ALL relevant files before reporting; maintain an internal checklist
- Base every finding on retrieved code only — never fabricate issues
- Cite exact file:line for each finding; mark blocked items with what information is missing
- Continue reading files until the review is complete; retry with different strategies if results are empty
- Verify each issue: confirm the problem exists at the cited location before reporting
- Do NOT spawn subagents or trigger additional review rounds — complete your entire review in a single response

1. **Blocking Issues**
無。

2. **Suggestions**
- [[Cee/Views/StatusBarView.swift:160](/Users/pc035860/code/cee/Cee/Views/StatusBarView.swift#L160)] 將 display-mode 門檻計算快取到文字或寬度真的變動時再重算 — 目前 `layout()` 每次都會重新量測 `full`/`compact` 兩段字串，而 pinch 路徑會經由 `updateZoom()` 持續設 `needsLayout`，高頻縮放時這段會變成熱路徑上的重複工作。
- [[CeeTests/ImageViewControllerTests.swift:61](/Users/pc035860/code/cee/CeeTests/ImageViewControllerTests.swift#L61)] 補一組 `compact` 與 hysteresis 邊界測試，並走 `updateZoom(_:)` 路徑 — 現有測試有覆蓋 `regular`/`minimal` 極端寬度與 formatter，但還沒鎖住 `compact` 進出條件，也沒直接覆蓋實際 pinch zoom 會走的更新方法，門檻微調後容易漏回歸。

3. **Questions**
無 — 目前沒有需要額外產品或行為決策才能判定對錯的項目。

4. **Summary**
整體改動乾淨，Auto Layout/fitting-size 方向也對，這次目標測試 18 項我跑過都通過了。沒看到必修 bug，主要剩熱路徑的小幅效能優化和 hysteresis 邊界測試可以再補強，嗯呢~

content.text:
# Simplify: Code Review and Cleanup

Review all changed files for reuse, quality, and efficiency. Fix any issues found.

## Phase 1: Identify Changes

Run `git diff` (or `git diff HEAD` if there are staged changes) to see what changed. If there are no git changes, review the most recently modified files that the user mentioned or that you edited earlier in this conversation.

## Phase 2: Launch Three Review Agents in Parallel

Use the Agent tool to launch all three agents concurrently in a single message. Pass each agent the full diff so it has the complete context.

### Agent 1: Code Reuse Review

For each change:

1. **Search for existing utilities and helpers** that could replace newly written code. Look for similar patterns elsewhere in the codebase — common locations are utility directories, shared modules, and files adjacent to the changed ones.
2. **Flag any new function that duplicates existing functionality.** Suggest the existing function to use instead.
3. **Flag any inline logic that could use an existing utility** — hand-rolled string manipulation, manual path handling, custom environment checks, ad-hoc type guards, and similar patterns are common candidates.

### Agent 2: Code Quality Review

Review the same changes for hacky patterns:

1. **Redundant state**: state that duplicates existing state, cached values that could be derived, observers/effects that could be direct calls
2. **Parameter sprawl**: adding new parameters to a function instead of generalizing or restructuring existing ones
3. **Copy-paste with slight variation**: near-duplicate code blocks that should be unified with a shared abstraction
4. **Leaky abstractions**: exposing internal details that should be encapsulated, or breaking existing abstraction boundaries
5. **Stringly-typed code**: using raw strings where constants, enums (string unions), or branded types already exist in the codebase
6. **Unnecessary JSX nesting**: wrapper Boxes/elements that add no layout value — check if inner component props (flexShrink, alignItems, etc.) already provide the needed behavior

### Agent 3: Efficiency Review

Review the same changes for efficiency:

1. **Unnecessary work**: redundant computations, repeated file reads, duplicate network/API calls, N+1 patterns
2. **Missed concurrency**: independent operations run sequentially when they could run in parallel
3. **Hot-path bloat**: new blocking work added to startup or per-request/per-render hot paths
4. **Recurring no-op updates**: state/store updates inside polling loops, intervals, or event handlers that fire unconditionally — add a change-detection guard so downstream consumers aren't notified when nothing changed. Also: if a wrapper function takes an updater/reducer callback, verify it honors same-reference returns (or whatever the "no change" signal is) — otherwise callers' early-return no-ops are silently defeated
5. **Unnecessary existence checks**: pre-checking file/resource existence before operating (TOCTOU anti-pattern) — operate directly and handle the error
6. **Memory**: unbounded data structures, missing cleanup, event listener leaks
7. **Overly broad operations**: reading entire files when only a portion is needed, loading all items when filtering for one

## Phase 3: Fix Issues

Wait for all three agents to complete. Aggregate their findings and fix each issue directly. If a finding is a false positive or not worth addressing, note it and move on — do not argue with the finding, just skip it.

When done, briefly summarize what was fixed (or confirm the code was already clean).

content.content:
1→import AppKit
     2→
     3→enum ZoomStatusMode: Equatable {
     4→    case fit
     5→    case actual(windowAuto: Bool)
     6→    case manual(percent: Int, windowAuto: Bool)
     7→}
     8→
     9→enum ZoomStatusStyle {
    10→    case full
    11→    case compactPercentOnly
    12→}
    13→
    14→enum ZoomStatusFormatter {
    15→    static func text(for mode: ZoomStatusMode, style: ZoomStatusStyle = .full) -> String {
    16→        switch style {
    17→        case .full:
    18→            return fullText(for: mode)
    19→        case .compactPercentOnly:
    20→            return compactText(for: mode)
    21→        }
    22→    }
    23→
    24→    private static func fullText(for mode: ZoomStatusMode) -> String {
    25→        let baseText: String
    26→        let windowAuto: Bool
    27→
    28→        switch mode {
    29→        case .fit:
    30→            return String(localized: "status.fit")
    31→        case .actual(let isWindowAuto):
    32→            baseText = String(localized: "status.actual") + " 100%"
    33→            windowAuto = isWindowAuto
    34→        case .manual(let percent, let isWindowAuto):
    35→            baseText = String(localized: "status.manual") + " \(percent)%"
    36→            windowAuto = isWindowAuto
    37→        }
    38→
    39→        guard windowAuto else { return baseText }
    40→        return baseText + " · " + String(localized: "status.windowAuto")
    41→    }
    42→
    43→    private static func compactText(for mode: ZoomStatusMode) -> String {
    44→        switch mode {
    45→        case .fit:
    46→            return String(localized: "status.fit")
    47→        case .actual:
    48→            return "100%"
    49→        case .manual(let percent, _):
    50→            return "\(percent)%"
    51→        }
    52→    }
    53→}

    55→final class StatusBarView: NSVisualEffectView {
    56→
    57→    enum DisplayMode {
    58→        case regular
    59→        case compact
    60→        case minimal
    61→    }
    62→
    63→    // MARK: - UI Elements
    64→
    65→    private let separator = NSBox()
    66→    private let sizeLabel = NSTextField(labelWithString: "")
    67→    private let indexLabel = NSTextField(labelWithString: "")
    68→    private let zoomLabel = NSTextField(labelWithString: "")
    69→
    70→    // MARK: - State
    71→
    72→    private var currentZoomMode: ZoomStatusMode?
    73→    private(set) var currentDisplayMode: DisplayMode = .regular
    74→
    75→    // MARK: - Constraints
    76→
    77→    private var zoomLabelConstraints: [NSLayoutConstraint] = []
    78→    private var minimalIndexTrailingConstraint: NSLayoutConstraint!
    79→
    80→    private static let contentPadding: CGFloat = 8
    81→    private static let labelGap: CGFloat = 4
    82→    private static let zoomGap: CGFloat = 8
    83→    private static let hysteresis: CGFloat = 20
    84→
    85→    // MARK: - Initialization
    86→
    87→    override init(frame frameRect: NSRect) {
    88→        super.init(frame: frameRect)
    89→        setupVisualEffect()
    90→        setupUI()
    91→    }
    92→
    93→    required init?(coder: NSCoder) {
    94→        super.init(coder: coder)
    95→        setupVisualEffect()
    96→        setupUI()
    97→    }
    98→
    99→    private func setupVisualEffect() {
   100→        material = .titlebar
   101→        blendingMode = .withinWindow
   102→        state = .active
   103→    }
   104→
   105→    private func setupUI() {
   106→        separator.boxType = .separator
   107→
   108→        [sizeLabel, indexLabel, zoomLabel].forEach { label in
   109→            label.font = .systemFont(ofSize: 11)
   110→            label.textColor = NSColor.secondaryLabelColor
   111→            label.alignment = .center
   112→            label.lineBreakMode = .byClipping
   113→        }
   114→
   115→        addSubview(separator)
   116→        addSubview(sizeLabel)
   117→        addSubview(indexLabel)
   118→        addSubview(zoomLabel)
   119→
   120→        separator.translatesAutoresizingMaskIntoConstraints = false
   121→        sizeLabel.translatesAutoresizingMaskIntoConstraints = false
   122→        indexLabel.translatesAutoresizingMaskIntoConstraints = false
   123→        zoomLabel.translatesAutoresizingMaskIntoConstraints = false
   124→
   125→        zoomLabel.setContentCompressionResistancePriority(.defaultLow, for: .horizontal)
   126→
   127→        let indexToZoom = indexLabel.trailingAnchor.constraint(
   128→            equalTo: zoomLabel.leadingAnchor, constant: -Self.zoomGap)
   129→        indexToZoom.priority = .defaultHigh
   130→
   131→        let zoomToTrailing = zoomLabel.trailingAnchor.constraint(
   132→            equalTo: trailingAnchor, constant: -Self.contentPadding)
   133→        zoomToTrailing.priority = .defaultHigh
   134→
   135→        zoomLabelConstraints = [indexToZoom, zoomToTrailing]
   136→
   137→        minimalIndexTrailingConstraint = indexLabel.trailingAnchor.constraint(
   138→            lessThanOrEqualTo: trailingAnchor, constant: -Self.contentPadding)
   139→        minimalIndexTrailingConstraint.isActive = false
   140→
   141→        NSLayoutConstraint.activate([
   142→            separator.topAnchor.constraint(equalTo: topAnchor),
   143→            separator.leadingAnchor.constraint(equalTo: leadingAnchor),
   144→            separator.trailingAnchor.constraint(equalTo: trailingAnchor),
   145→
   146→            sizeLabel.leadingAnchor.constraint(equalTo: leadingAnchor, constant: Self.contentPadding),
   147→            sizeLabel.centerYAnchor.constraint(equalTo: centerYAnchor),
   148→
   149→            sizeLabel.trailingAnchor.constraint(
   150→                lessThanOrEqualTo: indexLabel.leadingAnchor, constant: -Self.labelGap),
   151→
   152→            indexLabel.centerYAnchor.constraint(equalTo: centerYAnchor),
   153→
   154→            zoomLabel.centerYAnchor.constraint(equalTo: centerYAnchor),
   155→        ] + zoomLabelConstraints)
   156→    }
   157→
   158→    // MARK: - Layout
   159→
   160→    override func layout() {
   161→        super.layout()
   162→        let newMode = computeDisplayMode(for: bounds.width)
   163→        guard newMode != currentDisplayMode else { return }
   164→        applyDisplayMode(newMode)
   165→    }
   166→
   167→    private func computeDisplayMode(for width: CGFloat) -> DisplayMode {
   168→        guard let zoomMode = currentZoomMode else { return .regular }
   169→
   170→        let font = zoomLabel.font ?? .systemFont(ofSize: 11)
   171→        let coreWidth = Self.contentPadding
   172→            + sizeLabel.intrinsicContentSize.width
   173→            + Self.labelGap
   174→            + indexLabel.intrinsicContentSize.width
   175→            + Self.zoomGap
   176→
   177→        let fullText = ZoomStatusFormatter.text(for: zoomMode, style: .full)
   178→        let regularThreshold = coreWidth + textWidth(fullText, font: font) + Self.contentPadding
   179→
   180→        let compactText = ZoomStatusFormatter.text(for: zoomMode, style: .compactPercentOnly)
   181→        let compactThreshold = coreWidth + textWidth(compactText, font: font) + Self.contentPadding
   182→
   183→        switch currentDisplayMode {
   184→        case .regular:
   185→            if width < compactThreshold { return .minimal }
   186→            if width < regularThreshold { return .compact }
   187→            return .regular
   188→        case .compact:
   189→            if width < compactThreshold { return .minimal }
   190→            if width >= regularThreshold + Self.hysteresis { return .regular }
   191→            return .compact
   192→        case .minimal:
   193→            if width >= regularThreshold + Self.hysteresis { return .regular }
   194→            if width >= compactThreshold + Self.hysteresis { return .compact }
   195→            return .minimal
   196→        }
   197→    }
   198→
   199→    private func applyDisplayMode(_ mode: DisplayMode) {
   200→        currentDisplayMode = mode
   201→
   202→        switch mode {
   203→        case .regular, .compact:
   204→            zoomLabel.isHidden = false
   205→            NSLayoutConstraint.deactivate([minimalIndexTrailingConstraint])
   206→            NSLayoutConstraint.activate(zoomLabelConstraints)
   207→            applyZoomText()
   208→        case .minimal:
   209→            zoomLabel.isHidden = true
   210→            NSLayoutConstraint.deactivate(zoomLabelConstraints)
   211→            NSLayoutConstraint.activate([minimalIndexTrailingConstraint])
   212→        }
   213→    }
   214→
   215→    private func applyZoomText() {
   216→        guard let zoomMode = currentZoomMode else { return }
   217→        let style: ZoomStatusStyle = currentDisplayMode == .compact ? .compactPercentOnly : .full
   218→        zoomLabel.stringValue = ZoomStatusFormatter.text(for: zoomMode, style: style)
   219→    }
   220→
   221→    private func textWidth(_ text: String, font: NSFont) -> CGFloat {
   222→        ceil((text as NSString).size(withAttributes: [.font: font]).width)
   223→    }
   224→
   225→    // MARK: - Update Methods
   226→
   227→    func update(index: Int, total: Int, zoomMode: ZoomStatusMode, imageSize: NSSize,
   228→                indexOverride: String? = nil) {
   229→        indexLabel.stringValue = indexOverride ?? "\(index) / \(total)"
   230→        currentZoomMode = zoomMode
   231→        sizeLabel.stringValue = "\(Int(imageSize.width)) × \(Int(imageSize.height))"
   232→        applyZoomText()
   233→        needsLayout = true
   234→    }
   235→
   236→    func updateZoom(_ zoomMode: ZoomStatusMode) {
   237→        currentZoomMode = zoomMode
   238→        applyZoomText()
   239→        needsLayout = true
   240→    }
   241→
   242→    func updateIndex(current: Int, total: Int) {
   243→        indexLabel.stringValue = "\(current) / \(total)"
   244→    }
   245→
   246→    func clear() {
   247→        sizeLabel.stringValue = ""
   248→        indexLabel.stringValue = ""
   249→        zoomLabel.stringValue = ""
   250→        currentZoomMode = nil
   251→    }
   252→}
   253→

1→import AppKit
     2→
     3→enum ZoomStatusMode: Equatable {
     4→    case fit
     5→    case actual(windowAuto: Bool)
     6→    case manual(percent: Int, windowAuto: Bool)
     7→}
     8→
     9→enum ZoomStatusStyle {
    10→    case full
    11→    case compactPercentOnly
    12→}
    13→
    14→enum ZoomStatusFormatter {
    15→    static func text(for mode: ZoomStatusMode, style: ZoomStatusStyle = .full) -> String {
    16→        switch style {
    17→        case .full:
    18→            return fullText(for: mode)
    19→        case .compactPercentOnly:
    20→            return compactText(for: mode)
    21→        }
    22→    }
    23→
    24→    private static func fullText(for mode: ZoomStatusMode) -> String {
    25→        let baseText: String
    26→        let windowAuto: Bool
    27→
    28→        switch mode {
    29→        case .fit:
    30→            return String(localized: "status.fit")
    31→        case .actual(let isWindowAuto):
    32→            baseText = String(localized: "status.actual") + " 100%"
    33→            windowAuto = isWindowAuto
    34→        case .manual(let percent, let isWindowAuto):
    35→            baseText = String(localized: "status.manual") + " \(percent)%"
    36→            windowAuto = isWindowAuto
    37→        }
    38→
    39→        guard windowAuto else { return baseText }
    40→        return baseText + " · " + String(localized: "status.windowAuto")
    41→    }
    42→
    43→    private static func compactText(for mode: ZoomStatusMode) -> String {
    44→        switch mode {
    45→        case .fit:
    46→            return String(localized: "status.fit")
    47→        case .actual:
    48→            return "100%"
    49→        case .manual(let percent, _):
    50→            return "\(percent)%"
    51→        }
    52→    }
    53→}

1→import XCTest
     2→@testable import Cee
